channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Chip Roy: Trump wants Social Security benefits intact
item.description: Chip Roy believes Trump doesn't actually want to cut from Social Security and other essential benefits on which Americans rely.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Man accused in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing faces federal charge that's eligible for death penalty
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO was whisked back to New York by plane and helicopter Thursday to face new federal charges of stalking and murder, which could bring the death penalty if he's convicted.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, LARRY NEUMEISTER and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Farmers, business owners, fire survivors face uncertainty after $100B in disaster relief flounders
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — American farmers, small business owners and wildfire survivors are among those who will suffer if Congress cannot agree on a new spending bill after President-elect Donald Trump abruptly rejected a bipartisan plan that included more than $100 billion in disaster aid.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, LEAH WILLINGHAM and JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, Fox 5

item.title: Closures, Social Security checks, furloughs: What a government shutdown might mean
item.description: Congress has until midnight Friday to come up with a way to fund the government or federal agencies will shut down, meaning hundreds of thousands of federal employees could be sent home — or stay on the job without pay — just ahead of the holidays.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How drone activity could affect US holiday travel plans
item.description: Recent drone sightings around the country have raised concerns over whether drone activity could affect holiday travel
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chinese national charged with acting as Beijing's agent in local California election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Chinese national was arrested Thursday on charges of acting as an illegal agent for Beijing when serving as the campaign manager for an unnamed politician elected to a city council in Southern California two years ago.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, AMY TAXIN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Shutdown odds rapidly rise as House seeks plan B
item.description: The odds of a shutdown were rapidly growing on Thursday after President-elect Trump upended the talks by urging GOP lawmakers to shutter the government unless Democrats agree to a clean stopgap spending bill that raises or abolishes the debt limit.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Snow, ice and strong winds make for dangerous conditions in upper Midwest
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — A fast-moving winter storm brought snow, ice, strong winds and bitter cold to much of the upper Midwest, snarling traffic in the Twin Cities and forcing North Dakota officials to shut down an interstate highway.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Florida, a race is on to save the Everglades and protect a key source of drinking water
item.description: EVERGLADES, Fla. (AP) — In a region of Florida known as the River of Grass, John Kominoski plops into hip-deep waters. Blobs of brown periphyton – a mishmash of algae, bacteria and other organisms – carpet the surface.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and REBECCA BLACKWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 killed, 3 injured when freight train derails in small West Texas city after collision
item.description: Two Union Pacific employees were killed and three people were injured when a freight train collided with a tractor-trailer and derailed in a small West Texas city, authorities said Thursday. The train derailed around 5 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oklahoma man dies by lethal injection in the nation’s final execution of 2024
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — An Oklahoma man who killed a 10-year-old girl in a cannibalistic fantasy died by lethal injection Thursday in the nation’s 25th and final execution of the year. Kevin Ray Underwood was pronounced dead at 10:14 a.m. at the Oklahoma State Penitentiary in McAlester.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deer wearing hi-visibility vest spotted in Michigan
item.description: "There is literally a deer with an orange vest running around confused at the corner of Sterling and Moscow," Melissa Gow wrote on a Facebook post.
item.dc:creator: Duncan Phenix, NewsNation

item.title: South Dakota mall Santa gets phone, wallet stolen
item.description: "Someone broke into my dressing room at the Mall and took my Santa bag with my wallet and cell phone including my drivers (license) and all my credit cards," he said.
item.dc:creator: Gracie Terrall, NewsNation

item.title: FAA bans drones in parts of New Jersey
item.description: The FAA warned that "deadly force" could be used against the drones if they pose an "imminent security threat.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Former Uvalde schools police chief loses bid to toss criminal charges related to 2022 shooting
item.description: UVALDE, Texas (AP) — A Texas judge on Thursday refused to throw out criminal charges accusing the former Uvalde schools police chief of putting children at risk during the slow response to the 2022 Robb Elementary School shooting, while a lawyer for his co-defendant said they want to move the upcomi
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO will return to New York to face murder charges
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO will return to New York to face murder charges after agreeing to be extradited Thursday during a court appearance in Pennsylvania where he was arrested last week after five days on the run.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, ABC 4

item.title: Tiki, 20-year-old blind cat, saved in 'miracle' rescue from ice chunk in Massachusetts lake
item.description: A small black and white cat, who is blind, was rescued by good Samaritans after being stranded on a Massachusetts lake this week, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The egg industry kills 350 million male chicks a year. New tech offers an alternative
item.description: Every year the U.S. egg industry kills about 350 million male chicks because, while the fuzzy little animals are incredibly cute, they will never lay eggs, so have little monetary value.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Louisiana patient has first severe bird flu case in US: health officials
item.description: Human cases of bird flu have been reported in the U.S. before, but infections were mild in those patients.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, KRON4

item.title: NASA astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, already stuck in space, are hit with new delay
item.description: Astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, the crew of the first manned Boeing Starliner launch in early June, were initially meant to spend only about eight days in space.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Democracies across the globe are at a crossroads, as authoritarians seek to chip away at freedoms
item.description: In November, the world's most powerful democracy elected as its next president a man who schemed to overturn its last presidential election. A month later, South Koreans swarmed their legislature to block their president's attempt to impose martial law.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man convicted of quadruple homicide is put to death in Indiana's 1st execution in 15 years
item.description: MICHIGAN CITY, Ind. (AP) — An Indiana man convicted of killing four people including his brother and his sister’s fiancé decades ago was put to death early Wednesday, marking the state’s first execution in 15 years. Joseph Corcoran, 49, was pronounced dead at 12:44 a.m.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This Colorado restaurant only has 2 items on the menu
item.description: A Michelin-starred chef co-opened a restaurant in Denver with only two items on the menu: a burger and fries.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, Fox 5

item.title: Family of slain Cash App founder Bob Lee reacts to Nima Momeni verdict
item.description: On Tuesday morning, a San Francisco jury announced it had found Nima Momeni guilty of second degree murder in the death of Cash App founder Bob Lee.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, WGN 9

item.title: What we know about the victims in Wisconsin Christian school shooting
item.description: A 14-year-old girl and a substitute teacher were killed in Monday's shooting. Another teacher and five students were injured.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: College hockey player sued by opponent over injury she suffered on the ice
item.description: A Michigan State University hockey player has been sued by a former opponent for injuries she suffered on the ice.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Younger Asian Americans navigate something new to their generation: Taking up space
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The chairs stay occupied at 12 Pell. Client after client, they come through the tiny barbershop on a narrow side street in Manhattan’s Chinatown. They come for the cuts, sure. But really, they’re coming for the cool.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Teacher and a student killed in a shooting at a private Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A teenage student opened fire at the Abundant Life Christian School in Madison, Wisconsin on Monday, killing a teacher and another teenage student in the final week before Christmas break. The shooter also died, police said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Horse patrol keeps watch over 'roughest patch' of U.S.-Mexico border
item.description: LORDSBURG, New Mexico (Border Report) – Agency lore states that when Congress founded the U.S. Border Patrol in 1924, there were two requisites to join: Grab your gun and bring a horse.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Man sentenced for helping pregnant Chinese women travel to give birth in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California man was sentenced Monday to nearly 3 1/2 years in prison for running an extensive business that helped pregnant Chinese women travel to the United States to deliver babies who would automatically have American citizenship. U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 16-year-old killed entire family, called 911, police say
item.description: BELEN, N.M. (KRQE) – On Sunday, New Mexico State Police (NMSP) released the names of the four victims from Saturday's homicide in Belen. Police said that 16-year-old Diego Leyva called 911 early Saturday morning and told the dispatcher he had killed his family.
item.dc:creator: Scott Brown, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bud Light no longer the best-selling draft beer at bars, data suggests
item.description: The overtaking of Bud Light as America's most popular draft beer has been "years in the making," according to the founder of Draftline Technologies, a company that provides software for beverage distributors.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Jill Jacobson, actress who appeared on 'Star Trek' and 'Falcon Crest,' dies at 70
item.description: "We are incredibly sad to say goodbye to our beautiful, soulful, hysterically funny, elegantly raunchy client, Jill Jacobson," her manager Ben Padua told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, WGN 9
